page: Vultr vbm-128c-2048gb-amd is a bare metal server configured with 256 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7713 cores operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This server features a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, providing 256 vCPUs without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 960 GB of local SSD storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The bare metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, delivering direct hardware access to the AMD EPYC processors. With 2048.0 GB of RAM, this configuration is optimized for memory-intensive workloads that require massive physical memory footprints. The qualitative cost profile reflects a high-density resource allocation, trading off local storage capacity for massive compute and memory density.

This server is designed for large-scale enterprise workloads, including in-memory databases, heavy virtualization hosting, high-performance computing simulations, and complex data processing pipelines that demand substantial memory capacity and high parallel physical core execution.
description: Vultr vbm-128c-2048gb-amd is a high-density bare metal server featuring 256 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7713 cores running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Operating with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, the system provides 256 vCPUs of dedicated compute power. It is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 960 GB of local SSD storage. Designed without virtualization overhead, this bare metal instance delivers direct hardware access to support massive parallel processing. The resource profile is highly optimized for memory-intensive and compute-heavy enterprise applications, making it suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, virtualization hypervisors, and high-performance computing workloads.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal platform delivering two hundred fifty-six physical AMD cores and two terabytes of system memory.
